dynamic_variables.selected_variant_subtitle: All-gravel route from Dombas to Flam
dynamic_variables.selected_variant_stage_table:
Day 0 (2026-07-09): Arrive Oslo -> Oslo; 0 km; 0 m up; overnight Oslo.
Day 1 (2026-07-10): Dombas -> Vaga; 48 km; 900 m up; overnight Vaga.
Day 2 (2026-07-11): Vaga -> Haugseter (Vinstre); 90 km; 1750 m up; overnight Haugseter; hard day, start early.
Day 3 (2026-07-12): Haugseter -> Vaset; 77 km; 1000 m up; overnight Vaset; Sunday groceries caveat.
Day 4 (2026-07-13): Vaset -> Gol; 70 km; 800 m up; overnight Gol; recovery day and full-service resupply.
Day 5 (2026-07-14): Gol -> Haugastol; 75 km; 1300 m up; overnight Haugastol; Geilo is the last big resupply before Flam.
Day 6 (2026-07-15): Haugastol -> Flam; 83 km; 500 m up; overnight Flam.
Day 7 (2026-07-16): Flam -> Flam; side quest or rest day.
Day 8 (2026-07-17): Flam -> Bergen; travel day; be at the quay by 15:00 with a bike reservation.
Day 9 (2026-07-18): Bergen -> Oslo; train day; book the bike space.
Day 10 (2026-07-19): Oslo -> Fly home.
dynamic_variables.route_context_rule: Use the selected-variant stage table for day, date, and route-variant questions. Use tools for richer day detail, places, bailouts, notes, and uncertainty.

chat_history.message: Hello, how can I help you today?

success_condition: The agent recognizes the all-gravel or gravel variant and does not continue with the Besseggen day mapping. It explains the main sequencing changes, including no Besseggen hike and the all-gravel Day 4 being Vaset to Gol, or asks one concise clarification before comparing.
simulation_scenario: The simulated user opens by asking exactly: "What changes if I mean the all-gravel variant?" If the agent asks what to compare against, answer: "Compared with the Besseggen version."
name: FjordPilot 07 all-gravel variant change
